266c0d38dab0d0da: [{"severity": "MEDIUM", "confidence": 8, "path": "src/client.rs", "line": 15, "category": "red-team", "summary": "Port 443 is hardcoded without enforcing TLS, so callers previously connecting to non-TLS ports (e.g. 80, 8080, custom) silently get port 443 at runtime rather than a compile-time error, producing hard-to-diagnose connection failures instead of a clear break.", "fix": "If 443 must be the default, document the TLS requirement and enforce TLS in connect_with; otherwise keep a port parameter so callers explicitly choose."},{"severity": "LOW", "confidence": 9, "path": "src/client.rs", "line": 13, "category": "red-team", "summary": "A `// BUG:` comment explicitly documenting the defect is committed to source instead of the defect being fixed, permanently recording the known breakage in the codebase and misleading future readers about intentionality.", "fix": "Remove the self-annotating bug comment and fix the defect rather than narrating it."}]
3b9fdfa5749d9709: [{"severity": "HIGH", "confidence": 9, "path": "src/client.rs", "line": 15, "category": "error-handling", "summary": "`.unwrap()` on a fallible `connect_with` call in a public library function converts a connection failure into a panic instead of propagating the error to the caller.", "fix": "Restore the `Result<Client, Error>` return type and propagate the error with `?` rather than unwrapping."}]
7495f06fdeac0f47: [{"severity":"CRITICAL","confidence":10,"path":"src/client.rs","line":15,"category":"api-surface","summary":"connect() drops the port parameter and changes return type from Result<Client, Error> to Client, breaking every existing caller.","fix":"Restore the original signature pub fn connect(host: &str, port: u16) -> Result<Client, Error> or introduce a separate method for the new behavior."}]
